name: E-wing spine silhouette
description: Overall silhouette whose footprint forms an E: a long back spine with three projecting teeth (wings) reaching forward, enclosing two open light-courts between them. The Elizabethan-prodigy-house plan: the central tooth holds the porch and great hall, the flanking teeth hold lodgings, and the recesses flood the inner rooms with light. Consumed by the global_designer to fix the envelope before space_planner subdivides spine and teeth.
placement_rules: Footprint is a back spine with three forward teeth forming an E.
The central tooth carries the main entrance and the principal room.
The two recesses between teeth stay open as light-courts.
Teeth and spine share full walls; tooth width >= 4.

renaissance.notes: Prodigy-house E with a tall central porch tooth; symmetrical lodging wings.

medieval.notes: Stone-and-timber wings off a hall spine; gabled tooth ends.
